 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","title":"Rebuild the bvr TUI around the full FrankenTUI showcase contract before claiming parity","description":"Background:
The current TUI audit showed that `bvr` is still architecturally closer to a text-report renderer than to a flagship operator console. Most screens are composed from large multiline strings dropped into generic bordered paragraphs, while the repo documentation and parity ledger still imply that the TUI is largely complete. That mismatch is now itself a project risk.

Why this bead exists:
This epic is the canonical redesign track for turning the audit into executable work. The core requirement is not merely to make the Rust TUI prettier. It is to deliberately exploit the advanced capabilities already available in FrankenTUI/`ftui` so that `bvr` first recovers the workflow confidence and visual richness of legacy `bv`, and only then goes beyond it.

Non-negotiable design intent:
- Use every relevant high-end FrankenTUI capability that materially improves parity and operator effectiveness: global shell chrome, theme cycling, responsive layout tiers, pane geometry, hit-tested interaction regions, richer search affordances, semantic panels, stronger signal density, grapheme-safe text handling, terminal hyperlinks, and layout/visual diagnostics.
- Do not confuse a stable snapshot with a good product. The redesign must optimize for scanability, confidence, density, discoverability, and mode identity.
- Do not simplify away legacy workflows. Better-than-legacy improvements are welcome only after the legacy job-to-be-done is actually covered.

Scope of this epic:
1. Reset the TUI parity contract so the tracker and docs no longer overstate reality.
2. Build the missing shell, layout, visual, and interaction infrastructure required for a serious FrankenTUI implementation.
3. Rebuild the flagship main screen, then the board, graph, insights, and history surfaces around that infrastructure.
4. Add redesign-specific diagnostics and proof harnesses so future maintainers can validate quality without relying on tribal memory.

Acceptance:
- The child beads are sufficiently detailed that an implementer does not need to reopen `TUI_GAP_ANALYSIS.md` or prior planning docs to know what success means.
- The resulting implementation path is explicitly ordered: contract reset -> infrastructure -> flagship main screen -> secondary modes -> proof/evidence.
- Docs/verdict beads later in the tree depend on this redesign evidence instead of restating optimistic claims.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","parity","redesign","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:39.972459177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","title":"Reset the TUI parity contract around FrankenTUI-first redesign and evidence-based claims","description":"Purpose:
Convert the audit into a tracker-native contract that future agents can trust without reopening the markdown report. This bead is the truth-reset step for the TUI program.

Background and reasoning:
The project currently says the TUI is effectively complete, but the audit found a more serious reality: the app is only lightly using FrankenTUI and still behaves like a string-rendered report viewer in many places. If we skip this contract-reset step, future work will keep optimizing the wrong target and closing beads against the wrong standard.

Required scope:
- Capture in the issue tracker that the redesign must leverage the full relevant FrankenTUI showcase contract, not just `Paragraph` + `Block` + ad hoc color tokens.
- Name the required capability families explicitly: global shell, theme/discoverability, responsive layout tiers, hit-tested regions, adjustable panes, semantic panels, search excellence, signal density, hyperlinks, grapheme-safe text metrics, and visual diagnostics.
- Define what "parity first, then exceed legacy" means for TUI work so teams do not chase novelty before workflow recovery.

Acceptance:
- The tracker itself contains the redesign rationale, the bar, and the list of non-negotiable capabilities.
- Future implementation beads can reference this bead as the canonical intent document instead of relying on external markdown.
- This bead does not close until the rest of the redesign subtree is coherent and dependency-complete.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Treat this bead as the canonical tracker-native contract for the redesign, not a prose-only summary.\n2. Make the capability families explicit enough that later beads can be audited against them without reopening markdown planning docs.\n3. Include the user-outcome bar: recover legacy workflow confidence and look/feel first, then exceed it with FrankenTUI-native improvements.\n4. Ensure the redesign subtree contains an explicit proof story, not just implementation stories.\n\nFuture-self note: If this contract is vague, later beads will optimize for whatever looks finished instead of what actually serves operators.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The tracker-native redesign contract explicitly names the capability families, user-experience bar, and parity-first-then-beyond sequencing.\n2. The subtree is coherent enough that future agents can work from beads alone without reopening TUI_GAP_ANALYSIS.md.\n3. The proof obligations for unit/state, snapshots, keyflows, and shell-level journeys are represented somewhere under this subtree.\n4. Docs and verdict beads later in the tree depend on this contract and the evidence package rather than optimistic narrative.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.282959098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T03:05:20.577160487Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T03:05:20.576729742Z","close_reason":"Completed tracker-backed parity-contract reset and aligned repo-facing docs with the FrankenTUI-first redesign/proof program","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","frankentui","parity","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.282959098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","title":"Rebuild history mode as an investigation surface with event rail, commit cards, file-focus lens, and context-preserving cross-mode jumps","description":"Purpose:
Turn history mode into a serious investigative workspace rather than a long text report with multiple sections.

Background:
History mode is one of the richer current screens, but it is still constrained by the same string-first rendering model as everything else. The result is that the operator can technically inspect many things, yet the workflow still feels approximate instead of polished.

Required scope:
- Improve the visual structure of timelines, milestones, commits, related beads, and file drilldowns.
- Strengthen file-tree and related-bead workflows so they behave like an investigation tool rather than an add-on.
- Make cross-mode jumps preserve context and reduce operator disorientation.

Acceptance:
- History mode feels like a coherent investigation surface.
- The relationship between timeline, commit detail, file focus, and related-bead context is much clearer.
- Cross-mode continuity is better than the current approximation.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. If the rebuilt mode makes that harder, even with prettier visuals, it is a failure.","acceptance_criteria":"1.  {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1","title":"Redesign history timelines, milestone/event composition, and commit cards for fast investigation and chronology scanning","description":"Purpose:
Improve the core visual grammar of history mode so chronology and event significance are visible without reading dense text blocks.

Required scope:
- Rework event/timeline composition into a clearer rail or card-based chronology.
- Make milestone and lifecycle state changes more visually legible.
- Rebuild commit presentation so commit cards and change summaries are easier to scan and compare.

Acceptance:
- Chronology is easier to understand at a glance.
- Commit/event reading is faster and less text-fatiguing.
- The screen has stronger investigation rhythm than the current report-like output.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. History should read like a chronology tool, not a visually upgraded transcript.\n2. Event, milestone, and commit cards must remain comparable under dense timelines and history-rich projects.\n3. Sparse history, partial history, and ambiguous chronology need intentional presentation so the user understands what is missing.\n4. Responsive fallbacks must preserve time ordering and event significance even when there is no room for the richest layout.\n\nFuture-self note: If users still have to read line by line to reconstruct what happened, this bead is not succeeding.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Chronology is easier to understand at a glance.\n2. Commit/event reading is faster and less text-fatiguing across dense and sparse history cases.\n3. Partial or thin history is communicated honestly without destroying investigation rhythm.\n4. Snapshot, keyflow, and scenario-driven proof cover dense timelines, sparse histories, and responsive chronology layouts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:46.023159910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:34:40.403904895Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:34:40.403323906Z","close_reason":"History list enhanced: bead entries show status+type icons with ▸ marker, git commits show type icon from message (F/B/D/R/T/C) with compact duration timestamps.  {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11","title":"Add FrankenTUI-driven layout diagnostics and deterministic visual-debug tooling for the TUI redesign","description":"Purpose:
Make the redesign debuggable. Complex responsive TUI work should not rely on guesswork and snapshots alone.

Background:
The FrankenTUI showcase stack includes stronger layout-debug and deterministic-visual-debug patterns than `bvr` currently uses. The redesign will touch information architecture, widths, splitters, and hit regions across many screens, so the project needs better developer tools before it scales the redesign too far.

Required scope:
- Introduce or emulate layout-debugging aids that help explain why panels receive the sizes they do.
- Add deterministic visual-debug hooks suitable for testing complex screen states and responsive transitions.
- Make it easier to inspect hit regions, pane boundaries, or screen-mode thresholds while developing redesign work.

Acceptance:
- Developers have better-than-current tools for diagnosing wasted space, clipping, and awkward screen composition.
- The redesign is no longer validated only by staring at final snapshots.
- The tooling is practical enough to be reused across multiple screen rebuilds.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1.  {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1","title":"Redesign main-screen rows with dense scan-line chips, rank signals, blocker context, and stronger information scent","description":"Purpose:

Required scope:
- Replace the current low-information row format with a compact but legible scan line built from semantic chips/badges and aligned signals.
- Surface enough context that a user can compare items without repeatedly opening the detail pane.
- Preserve readability at narrow widths by using responsive row variants rather than just truncating everything.

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","title":"Recompose the main detail pane into a modular cockpit: summary, action state, dependencies, metrics, and narrative","description":"Purpose:
Replace the current line-oriented report in the main detail pane with a modular, high-hierarchy layout that makes actionability and context obvious.

Why this matters:
The current detail pane has valuable data, but it is arranged like a narrative dump. Operators should be able to understand state, blockers, risk, and recommended action from a small number of visually distinct modules.

Required scope:
- Create a high-priority summary module that immediately answers: what is this, why does it matter, and what should I do next?
- Separate action state, dependencies, graph signals, and narrative/body content into visually distinct groups.
- Use semantic panels, badges, metric strips, and hierarchy rather than relying on whitespace and colons alone.
- Keep rich long-form fields readable without letting them dominate the top of the pane.

Acceptance:
- The top half of the detail pane communicates the core state/action story much faster than the current design.
- Rich narrative sections still exist, but they no longer crowd out the operational summary.
- The detail pane feels like a decision cockpit rather than a report body.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. The top modules must answer what this is, why it matters now, and what to do next before the user reads long narrative text.\n2. Make dependency, risk, and action cues scannable enough that the pane helps with prioritization rather than only documentation.\n3. Handle sparse metadata and overlong narrative gracefully so the pane still feels intentional when fields are missing or huge.\n4. Preserve drill links and adjacent-workflow handoffs where they improve operator flow.\n\nFuture-self note: This pane should still be useful when data is messy, incomplete, or verbose. If it only looks good on perfect issues, it is not done.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The top half of the detail pane communicates the core state/action story much faster than the current design.\n2. Rich narrative sections still exist, but they no longer crowd out the operational summary.\n3. Sparse, missing, and extremely verbose fields degrade gracefully without destroying hierarchy or readability.\n4. Unit, snapshot, and journey/keyflow coverage prove both normal and degraded detail-pane states, including long content and linked handoffs.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.387066833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:08:59.032299697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["detail","frankentui","main-view","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.180090767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:51.562731182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:42.387066833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3","title":"Overhaul main-screen search, filter, focus, and discoverability so the default workflow feels immediate","description":"Purpose:
Make the main screen fast and legible for both first-time and expert use by upgrading its interaction affordances, not just its visuals.

Required scope:
- Apply the full search-excellence contract to the main screen: search-as-you-type, match counts, stronger active-match emphasis, and better context than a bare query banner.
- Improve filter visibility so active filters, label/repo scopes, and search state are obvious in the shell and screen body.
- Improve focus signaling between list, detail, and any auxiliary controls so users are not forced to infer focus from subtle cursor movement.
- Reduce dependence on long footer prose by surfacing action/discovery cues nearer to the relevant UI regions.

Acceptance:
- Main-view search/filter/focus behavior feels productized rather than bolted on.
- Search results are easier to understand and cycle through than in the current implementation.
- A first-time operator can infer more of the workflow from the interface itself.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Optimize for the first 30 seconds of use: users should understand search, filter, match navigation, and focus ownership from the interface itself.\n2. Treat no-hit and over-filtered states as first-class UX surfaces with clear recovery cues, not as edge leftovers.\n3. Keep expert workflows fast while making novice recovery obvious when focus, filters, or search state become confusing.\n4. Make search/focus behavior deterministic enough that the lower-level and E2E proof beads can assert it precisely.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is where the main view stops feeling hostile to first-time users.  {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","title":"Rebuild insights mode as a responsive analytics console with stronger visual identity and outlier surfacing","description":"Purpose:
Turn insights mode into a genuine analysis console rather than a sequence of textual analytics panels.

Background:
The current insights mode has useful computations, but the mode identity is weak because the information is still presented mostly as text. That undercuts the sense that this view is where a user comes to understand the system, spot anomalies, and decide where to dig deeper.

Required scope:
- Use tiles, metric clusters, heatmaps, ranked outlier modules, and stronger section framing rather than relying on one long textual panel body.
- Make responsive visibility decisions so the most valuable insight modules survive on smaller terminals.
- Preserve explanation/proof affordances while upgrading the visual analytics layer.

Acceptance:
- Insights mode has a clear analytics-console identity.
- High-signal findings are easier to spot at a glance.
- The mode balances explainability with visual density.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Insights mode must become visually distinct from all report-like surfaces.\n2. Keep explanation and proof affordances, but organize them under a stronger analytics-console IA.\n3. Use responsive visibility rules so high-value insight modules remain visible when space is tight.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is where the product starts to feel like an advanced triage engine rather than just a viewer with extra screens.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Insights mode has a clear analytics-console identity with stronger outlier surfacing and visual hierarchy.\n2. Explanation and proof behavior still exists and remains testable.\n3.  {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.1","title":"Rebuild insights mode around analytic tiles, outlier surfacing, and stronger at-a-glance signal density","description":"Purpose:
Make insights mode visually and operationally distinct by turning it into an analytics surface instead of a rotating text report.

Required scope:
1. Introduce tile/grid-style analytic modules where appropriate.
2. Surface outliers, bottlenecks, and unusual patterns more aggressively than the current mode does.
3. Use stronger metric visuals and section hierarchy so high-value signals are obvious quickly.

Acceptance:
1. The first glance at insights mode communicates analysis, not prose.
2. Operators can identify notable patterns faster than in the current implementation.
3. Snapshot/journey evidence proves the new density and hierarchy across widths.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. The first screenful must reveal the important pattern or anomaly before the user starts reading explanatory prose.\n2. Tile and grid density should remain meaningful under wide, medium, and narrow layouts instead of degenerating into unlabeled clutter.\n3. Empty, sparse, or low-signal analytical states need intentional presentation so the mode stays trustworthy even when there is little to show.\n4. Stronger density must not hide provenance; important tiles should still signal where the number came from or where to drill next.\n\nFuture-self note: If insights only looks busier rather than more revealing, the redesign is superficial.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The first glance at insights mode communicates analysis, not prose.\n2. Operators can identify notable patterns faster than in the current implementation, including under sparse and low-signal datasets.\n3. Snapshot, journey, and scenario-driven proof demonstrate density and hierarchy across widths, including empty/fallback analytical states.\n4. High-value tiles remain interpretable enough to support drilldown rather than becoming ornamental numbers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.596462923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:49.782071183Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","density","frankentui","insights","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:54.596462923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.2","title":"Preserve explainability while adding responsive visibility rules and drill paths to the rebuilt insights console","description":"Purpose:
Ensure the redesigned insights screen remains trustworthy and explorable, not just visually denser.

Required scope:
1. Preserve or improve the existing explanation and proof affordances.
2. Add responsive visibility rules so important modules survive small screens while lower-value detail degrades gracefully.
3. Add drill paths from at-a-glance insight modules into deeper detail or adjacent workflows.

Acceptance:
1. Insights mode remains explainable and navigable while becoming denser and more visual.
2. Narrow and tiny layouts still preserve the most important insight modules.
3. Unit/state and journey tests cover explainability and drill workflows.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Explanations must stay close enough to the visual modules that users can trust what they are seeing without hunting for meaning.\n2. Drill paths should preserve context and avoid dumping users into unrelated screens without explanation.\n3. Responsive visibility rules must rank modules by operator value, not by implementation convenience.\n4. Low-data and partially computed states should remain transparent about what is missing or approximate.\n\nFuture-self note: A denser insights screen that users do not trust is worse than the current one. Explainability and provenance are part of the product, not add-ons.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Insights mode remains explainable and navigable while becoming denser and more visual.\n2. Narrow and tiny layouts still preserve the most important insight modules and clearly communicate omitted or approximate detail.\n3. Drill paths preserve user context and make sense from the originating module.\n4. Unit/state, journey, and scenario-driven proof cover explainability, drill workflows, and low-data/partial-compute states with detailed logging.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.000135865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:55.953644186Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["explainability","frankentui","insights","responsive","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.000135865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
